pre_assessment.planned_strategy: Multi-phase: (1) call lemma_reduce_boundaries for bounds, (2) by(bit_vector) for identity case, (3) mask/shift → mod/div conversion + algebraic regrouping for value identity, (4) derive mod-p from value identity
stated_uncertainties: Whether I can complete the algebraic regrouping in 2 attempts
Type mismatches with mask/shift lemma signatures
Whether the solver needs per-limb intermediate assertions

attempt_1.code: [Partial: ensures #1 (bounds via lemma_reduce_boundaries) + ensures #2 (identity via by(bit_vector)) + attempt at ensures #3 setup (mask→mod, shift→div, fundamental_div_mod). Used admit() for remaining algebraic identity.]
attempt_1.error_message: Type mismatch errors: lemma_u64_low_bits_mask_is_mod and lemma_u64_shr_is_div have different signatures than expected. Also used wrong function name initially (lemma_u64_and_mask_is_mod).

post_assessment.stated_surprises: Even the setup for the algebraic identity (converting masks/shifts to mod/div) ran into type issues. The actual regrouping would require 50+ lines of intermediate assertions connecting the expanded spec_reduce terms to u64_5_as_nat. This proof is fundamentally a multi-page algebraic derivation that needs careful intermediate steps.
post_assessment.alternate_strategy: Would need to: (1) fix type issues with mask/shift lemma signatures, (2) explicitly expand u64_5_as_nat for both sides, (3) use fundamental_div_mod per limb, (4) regroup terms with explicit distributivity/associativity/commutativity lemmas, (5) identify the telescoping pattern where carries cancel. Likely needs 70+ lines of intermediate assertions.

diagnosis.strategy_detail: The multi-phase approach is correct: bounds → identity → value → mod. The strategy matches what the GT likely does.
diagnosis.lemma_detail: Found the right lemmas (lemma_reduce_boundaries, mask/shift lemmas, fundamental_div_mod). Got one name wrong initially.
diagnosis.instantiation_detail: Type mismatches with mask/shift lemma arguments — unclear on exact function signatures from the macro-generated code
diagnosis.verus_detail: Correct use of assert forall, by(bit_vector) with requires and let-bindings, =~= for array equality
diagnosis.under_detail: Only completed ensures #1 and #2 setup; ensures #3 (the main algebraic identity) was not attempted beyond initial setup due to complexity
